<center>

::: {style="text-align: center !important; width: 100%;"}
<h1 style="text-align: center !important;">

Welcome to LISSY !

</h1>
:::

</center>

![](/images/intro_console.png){fig-align="center"}

A remote-execution system that allows [Luxembourg Income Study (LIS)](https://www.lisdatacenter.org/) users to access LIS and LWS microdata from anywhere, using four different statistical software packages (**Stata**, **SAS**, **SPSS**, and **R**)

::: {style="text-align: center; margin-top: 20px;"}
<a href="https://webui.lisdatacenter.org/userinterface/" 
     style="
       display: inline-block;
       padding: 12px 28px;
       background-color: #325d88;
       color: white;
       text-decoration: none;
       border-radius: 25px;
       font-size: 16px;
       font-weight: 500;
     "> Login to LISSY </a>
:::

This user guide is organized into three main sections to support users depending on their level of experience and preferred working environment.

For new users, head to the **Getting Started** section, where you will find all the essential information on how to [register for LISSY](/getting-started/registration.html) and how to [submit your first jobs](/getting-started/submit_first_job.html) using the LISSY interface.

For users already familiar with the platform, or looking to deepen their understanding of LIS data workflows and follow best practices, you can directly explore the dedicated sections for **[R Users](/r-users/index.html)** and **[Stata Users](/stata-users/index.html)**, depending on the statistical software you use.


In case of any doubts, please reach us via our [User Support](/faqs/index.qmd#contact-support) service.

**News and Upcoming Events:**

- Most recent activity at [LIS](https://www.lisdatacenter.org/news-and-events/)
- For the latest [Data News](https://www.lisdatacenter.org/news-and-events/highlights/)
- Check here the latest releases of our quarterly [newsletter](https://www.lisdatacenter.org/newsletter/)
